body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes
applicable: yes (capability) — but driver_gap (no recording rig)

## Reasoning

`multiple-sessions-same-cwd` (scenario-meanings.md): two instances of the SAME agent launched in one working directory must appear as two distinct session rows, each with its own session_id and PID, transitioning state independently with no flapping and no PID cross-contamination. Primitive exercised = PID-based session isolation. Explicitly NOT `session-resume` (one session_id reused across PID lifetimes) and NOT `multiple-agents-same-workspace` (different agent types).

**agent_supports: yes.** Codex CLI 0.130.0 supports launching multiple concurrent `codex` processes in the same cwd as-shipped. Each codex process mints its own rollout UUID and writes to a distinct transcript under `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/<rollout-uuid>.jsonl` (adapter.go: `defaultRootDir = .codex/sessions`, FilesUnderRoot). The rollout path is keyed by date+UUID, not cwd, so two codex sessions in the same cwd produce two distinct rollouts. (capabilities.json records `session_id_assignment: false` — codex mints its own UUID rather than accepting `--session-id` — but that does not block running two instances; it only means the recipe cannot pin the UUIDs in advance.)

**irrlicht_observes: yes.** This is the load-bearing verdict and it is actually STRONGER for codex than for claudecode. The codex Source is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.codex/sessions/, Parser: JSONLineParser{NewParser: () => &Parser{}}}` — a fresh stateful `Parser{}` per rollout file, so two sessions in one cwd never share parser state (no metric/turn cross-contamination). The fswatcher picks up two new rollout files (one per UUID) → two SessionState rows. PID-to-session binding is the key seam: codex's `DiscoverPID` (pid.go) calls `processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByTranscriptWriter(transcriptPath)` — it finds the owning PID by which process currently holds THAT specific rollout file open for writing (codex keeps its rollout open for the session lifetime). This binds each session to its own PID by per-file ownership, NOT by the cwd+start-time heuristic claudecode uses — so two concurrent codex processes in the same cwd are disambiguated by which rollout each holds open, with no cwd-based ambiguity. Spec bullets map cleanly: distinct session_id+PID (two rollouts → two rows, each PID from its file-writer), independent transitions (per-file tailer goroutine + per-file Parser), stable PID binding with no cross-contamination (binding persists from file-writer discovery until process_exited).

## What blocks a recording (driver_gap, not a verdict problem)

The capability is fully supported by both codex and irrlicht (confidence 0.9), mirroring the claudecode cell (confidence 0.9, same driver_gap). The blocker is purely the recording rig: the codex interactive driver (`scripts/drive-codex-interactive.sh`) launches exactly ONE tmux/codex process per run and every step type (send/wait_turn/interrupt/keys/sleep/reset_session/fork/exit_clean/resume) operates on that single `$SESSION`. Its `session.uuids` / `transcript.paths` multi-session lists capture SERIAL session swaps within one process (a `/clear` or `/fork` mints a new rollout that the driver re-discovers) — NOT two CONCURRENTLY-LIVE codex processes. There is no `parallel_session` / `tmux_split` step that can spawn a second codex in the same cwd while the first stays alive and route `send`/`wait_turn` to a named target. Without two simultaneously-live processes there is nothing for `DiscoverPIDByTranscriptWriter` to disambiguate, which is the entire point of the scenario. Recording requires a driver extension; we do NOT fabricate concurrency.

## Conclusion

The cell is capability-applicable (agent_supports=yes, irrlicht_observes=yes) but frozen as a driver_gap: `by_adapter.codex.applicable=false` with a scope_note naming the missing concurrent-second-session primitive. expected.jsonl is preserved as the documented forward-compatible target (the two-row, two-PID arc). Unblocks once the codex interactive driver gains a `parallel_session` / `tmux_split` step type that can launch a second concurrent codex in the same cwd and route send/wait_turn to a named target. This is the same blocker recorded on the claudecode cell.
caveats: Recording recipe must launch two concurrent `codex` processes in the same cwd and drive them in parallel (tmux split panes or two named tmux sessions sharing the recording daemon). The current single-`$SESSION` driver serializes everything, so it cannot express two simultaneously-live processes — this is the recording-rig gap, not a codex or irrlicht limitation.

DiscoverPIDByTranscriptWriter is the critical seam: it binds by which process holds each rollout file open for writing. If both codex processes start within the same scanner tick before either has flushed its first rollout line, the file-writer probe may briefly find no owner; a future phase contract should anchor PID-to-session checks at a moment when both rollouts have written at least one line.

codex mints its own rollout UUID (session_id_assignment: false), so unlike claudecode the recipe cannot pre-pin the two UUIDs via a flag — the spec must assert two DISTINCT new_session UUIDs discovered from the rollout dir, not two known IDs.

sources.note: multiple-sessions-same-cwd: two instances of the same agent in one cwd → two distinct session_id+PID rows, independent transitions, stable non-cross-contaminating PID binding; primitive = PID-based session isolation; distinct from session-resume and multiple-agents-same-workspace.

Source: FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.codex/sessions/, Parser: JSONLineParser{NewParser: () => &Parser{}}} — a fresh stateful parser per rollout file means two codex sessions in one cwd do not share parser state.

DiscoverPID delegates to processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByTranscriptWriter(transcriptPath): binds each session to the PID holding ITS rollout file open for writing — stronger per-file isolation than claudecode's cwd+start-time heuristic; two concurrent codex in one cwd are disambiguated by file ownership, not cwd.
defaultRootDir = .codex/sessions; rollouts live under sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/<uuid>.jsonl keyed by date+UUID, not cwd — two same-cwd sessions yield two distinct rollout files.
headless_mode: true (scenario requires it); session_id_assignment: false (codex mints its own UUID — recipe cannot pre-pin the two IDs).

Single-process driver: one tmux/codex per run, one $SESSION, step types send/wait_turn/interrupt/keys/sleep/reset_session/fork/exit_clean/resume. session.uuids/transcript.paths capture serial /clear+/fork session swaps within one process, NOT two concurrently-live processes. No parallel_session/tmux_split primitive — the driver_gap.
Sibling claudecode cell: same capability verdict (yes/yes, confidence 0.9) and same driver_gap blocker — recording needs multi-process tmux orchestration not yet in the driver.
